Title: Transgenic soybean overexpressing GmSAMT1 exhibits resistance to multiple-HG types of soybean cyst nematode Heterodera glycines

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SABATH methyltransferase family; salicylic acid methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to salicylic acid to form methyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and remodels salicylic acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling associated with systemic acquired resistance and nematode resistance responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves analyzed for salicylic acid, benzoic acid and phenylalanine; roots were the transgene expression site relevant to SCN resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endogenous GmSAMT1 transcripts detected in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 35S-driven overexpression in roots; total root transcript abundance varied among independent transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SCN inoculation with HG type 1.2.5.7, HG type 0, and HG type 2.5.7; greenhouse temperature regimes of 22°C and warmer 27/23°C; field conditions with negligible SCN pressure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduced female index of multiple SCN HG types, decreased leaf salicylic acid in some lines, showed decreased benzoic acid trend, and increased phenylalanine without yield penalty under tested field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmSAMT1 overexpression in stable transgenic soybean conferred significant resistance to SCN HG type 1.2.5.7, HG type 0, and HG type 2.5.7. 2. Higher root GmSAMT1 expression was negatively correlated with female index for HG type 0. 3. The highest expressing line showed about half the leaf salicylic acid level of control, benzoic acid trended lower, and phenylalanine increased strongly. 4. No significant yield penalty was detected in a low-SCN field trial, and warmer greenhouse conditions suggested possible yield benefit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in soybean; qRT-PCR; SCN bioassay using female index; GC-MS metabolite profiling; correlation analysis; field and greenhouse agronomic evaluation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant negative effects on morphology or seed yield under tested field conditions; warmer greenhouse conditions suggested increased rather than decreased yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for cisgenic soybean improvement to enhance broad SCN resistance with minimal agronomic penalty; expression tuning may optimize resistance across HG types
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by GC-MS; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No methyl salicylate was detected in leaves or roots, possibly due to volatile emission. PR1 and PR3 steady-state transcript levels were unchanged, suggesting resistance was not mediated through altered basal PR expression. Source species and applied species are the same in this study.
